summary: This is a very brief meta-post where Joel notices a spike in his blog's readership and investigates the source. He discovers that The Motley Fool linked to his site, explaining the traffic increase. The post is essentially a public note-to-self about referral traffic.
key_points: Joel tracked his readership using Userland's 'most read sites' ranking
A link from The Motley Fool drove a significant traffic spike to Joel on Software
Early blogging culture involved publicly acknowledging and investigating inbound links
money_quotes: Somebody must be linking to this site; my readership is up dramatically since yesterday.
key_insight: A brief housekeeping post reflecting the early blogosphere's culture of tracking and acknowledging referral traffic sources.
